title: SAT Physics Mastery Challenge
description: Sharpen your physics skills with 20 SAT-style questions from mechanics to thermodynamics. Time is ticking!

questions.questionText: A 2 kg object is dropped from a height of 20 meters. Ignoring air resistance, what is its velocity just before hitting the ground?
Which of the following particles has no electric charge?
questions.explanation: Neutrons are neutral, electrons and protons are charged.

questions.questionText: What is the electric potential energy of a 2 C charge placed in a 12 V electric field?
If the frequency of a wave is doubled while its speed remains constant, what happens to the wavelength?
questions.explanation: v = fλ → if f doubles, λ must halve to keep v constant.

questions.questionText: A convex lens forms an image that is real, inverted, and larger than the object. Where is the object placed?
questions.explanation: This condition occurs when the object is between F and 2F from the lens.

questions.questionText: What is the total resistance of three 6-ohm resistors in parallel?
What happens to a gas’s pressure if the volume is halved while temperature remains constant?
questions.explanation: Boyle’s Law: P ∝ 1/V → halve volume, pressure doubles.

questions.questionText: A 5 kg object moves in a circle of radius 2 m with speed 4 m/s. What is the centripetal force?
The energy of a photon is directly proportional to which property?
questions.explanation: E = hf, so energy is proportional to frequency.

questions.questionText: Which subatomic particle is emitted during beta decay?
questions.explanation: Beta decay releases electrons (beta particles).

questions.questionText: What is the root mean square (RMS) speed of gas molecules related to?
questions.explanation: v_rms ∝ √(T/M) so it depends on temperature and molecular mass.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
questions.explanation: Energy is scalar; others are vectors with direction.

questions.questionText: Which condition must be met for total internal reflection to occur?
options: A) Light travels from air to water
C) Light travels from denser to rarer medium at critical angle
questions.explanation: TIR only occurs from denser to rarer medium at or above critical angle.

questions.questionText: A wire carries 3 A current. How much charge flows through it in 10 seconds?
A transverse wave on a string has a frequency of 5 Hz and a wavelength of 2 meters. What is its speed?
A 3 μC charge is placed in an electric field of strength 2×10³ N/C. What is the force on it?
The Doppler effect causes a sound to appear ______ if the source is moving toward the listener.
questions.explanation: Approaching sound has higher frequency = higher pitch.

questions.questionText: Which principle explains lift in airplane wings?
questions.explanation: Bernoulli’s principle: faster air over wing = lower pressure = lift.

questions.questionText: What quantity is conserved in all types of collisions?
questions.explanation: Momentum is conserved in all collisions; KE is not always.

questions.questionText: What does the slope of a position vs. time graph represent?
questions.explanation: Slope of position-time graph = velocity.

rewards.description: Earn 1200 XP for completing this physics challenge!
